creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Austin Shafer from comment #5)

The machine is a Dell Precision M4800 with a 4K panel.  I have updated the BIOS to the most recent revision.  Apparently this machine is also available in a dual graphics configuration, but with a 4K panel, the internal graphics are disabled and only the MXM module graphics are used.

The machine originall shipped with an nvidia Quadro K2100M which ran fine with nvidia-driver-340 and sc(4), though it had the same issues when ran with a more recent nvidia driver and vt(4).  I had previously ignored these issue as downgrading to nvidia-driver-340 was good enough of a fix.

However, nvidia-driver-340 does not support some newer OpenGL standards like GLES which caused more and more problems, so I recently swapped the GPU for an Nvidia Quadro M2200.  This one now needs a more recent graphics driver, causing all the problems I described.

The machine currently boots with legacy boot as I hadn't configured EFI in the original installation.  I have tried all permutations of loading nvidia{,-modeset,-drm} from loader.conf and rc.conf with no discernable difference.  Using/not using drm seems to change some things, but it's not clear what.  Of course, vt(4) is used.

Due to an unrelated issue, the Wifi card only works once the system has been suspended and then woken up.  So my current startup routine is:

 1. boot the system
 2. log in to the text console
 3. execute zzz(8), wait for the system to suspend
 4. push the power button, wait for the system to wakeup (screen stays blank or some times shows graphics glitches)
 5. blindly type "startx" to make an X server come up
 6. X server now works, but text console won't

If I suspend the system while X is running, the screen too stays blank on wakeup.  In some configurations I found that I could get the system back to working by blindly starting another X server on a different console, but it doesn't seem to always work.  Even then, it looks like some times the contents of graphics buffers are destroyed (e.g. the background image becomes white noise).  I had one configuration where this worked, but I am unable to get back to it and I forgot what it was specifically.  I have performed updates of all driver packages to the most recent versions in the meanwhile, so perhaps that changed things.
